seoTitle: Use Monday.com for Event Management (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Plan events in Monday.com with registration forms, vendor tracking, budget widgets, and day-of Gantt timelines for full team coordination.
intro: Coordinating vendors, registrations, and day-of logistics for an event usually means juggling too many spreadsheets and email threads. This guide shows how to build an event management board in Monday.com, from structuring pre-event planning through post-event wrap-up, collecting registrations with a form, and giving stakeholders a dashboard view of budget and progress.

steps.heading: Create your event board
steps.body: Structure group layouts such as Pre-Event Planning, Day-of Logistics, and Post-Event Wrap-up.

steps.heading: Add planning columns
steps.body: Use Timeline, Status, People, and Numbers columns to track task ownership, deadlines, and real-time completions.

steps.heading: Collect registrations with a form
steps.body: Set up the Monday.com Form View to collect attendee registrations or vendor RSVPs directly into your board.

steps.heading: Automate status notifications
steps.body: Build automations to notify team members when a task shifts to "Stuck" or a milestone deadline is approaching.

steps.heading: Map day-of logistics
steps.body: Switch to Gantt and Calendar views to map out event day logistics and speaker or vendor schedules.

steps.heading: Build an event dashboard
steps.body: Design a dashboard with budget and workload widgets for high-level stakeholder visibility.

faq.question: Can attendees register without using Monday.com directly?
faq.answer: Yes, the Form View lets you collect attendee registrations or vendor RSVPs that pipe automatically into your board.

faq.question: How do I know if a task is falling behind schedule?
faq.answer: Automations can notify team members when a task shifts to "Stuck" or when a milestone deadline is approaching.

faq.question: Can I manage multi-track schedules and speaker timelines?
faq.answer: Yes, switching to Gantt and Calendar views helps map out event day logistics including speaker schedules and multi-track agendas.

faq.question: Can stakeholders see budget status without editing the board?
faq.answer: Yes, a dashboard with budget and workload widgets gives high-level stakeholders visibility without needing edit access.
